title: Clinogyne holostachya
taxonomy: Clinogyne holostachya belongs to the family Compositae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. It is a type of plant native to tropical Africa, and its classification is further detailed at the genus level as part of the tribe Inuleae. The plant's classification is also known as a dicotyledon, indicating that it has two cotyledons or seed leaves. Within the plant kingdom, Clinogyne holostachya falls under the kingdom Plantae. Its classification as a flowering plant also indicates that it reproduces through the production of flowers and seeds.
uses: Clinogyne holostachya is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research has shown that it may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. However, more in-depth studies are needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential health benefits. Further research is necessary to explore the plant's full therapeutic potential.

intro: Clinogyne holostachya, also known as Clinogyne, is a plant species native to various regions of Africa and Asia, characterized by its small, yellowish flowers and aromatic leaves. Clinogyne holostachya is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Clinogyne is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, with the latter being used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and relieve stress.
